column1: # Baltic Sea equilibrium biomass targets for calibration
# Biomass type: total stock biomass (not SSB)
# These are order-of-magnitude targets with acceptable ranges for calibration
# Cod: ICES confirms eastern SSB below Blim (98 kt) throughout 2018-2022. Target
#   aggregates eastern (SD24-32) + western (SD22-24) stocks. Post-2015 collapse documented.
# Herring: 1.5 Mt is aggregate across all Baltic herring management units (central
#   SSB alone ~400-500 kt. Aggregation across independently assessed stocks is a
#   modeling simplification — spatial distribution differs between stocks.
# Sprat: ICES confirms SSB above Blim; ~1 Mt SSB plausible for SD22-32.
#   DOI 10.17895/ices.advice.19453856 is the ICES sprat (not cod) advice sheet.
# Stickleback: Olsson et al. 2019 (doi:10.1093/icesjms/fsz078) confirmed — first
#   large-scale biomass assessment. Wide range appropriate given boom-bust dynamics.

column7: ICES SD24-32 eastern+western Baltic 2018-2022; eastern SSB ~60-77kt post-collapse
column8: Post-2015 collapse state; total biomass ~1.5-2x SSB

column7: ICES aggregate Baltic herring complex (SD22-32 incl. Gulf of Bothnia)
column8: Central Baltic SSB ~450kt; total across all stocks ~1-2Mt

column7: ICES Baltic sprat SD22-32; SSB ~1.0Mt in 2022 (doi:10.17895/ices.advice.19453856)
column8: Total biomass > SSB; raised from 800kt to match ICES
Poorly assessed at basin scale; coarse grid under-resolves habitat
Concentrated in estuaries/lagoons; coarse grid under-resolves

column7: Baltic smelt (Osmerus eperlanus) — widely distributed; dominant forage fish in Gulf of Bothnia/Finland
